mapping.a_element: winding string in an excited (non-zero) mode as the coherent object
mapping.b_element: incident soliton as the coherent object

mapping.a_element: the compactified Milne singularity (contraction-to-a-point then re-expansion) as the localized feature encountered
mapping.b_element: the attractive delta-function impurity as the localized feature encountered

brief_justification: The only overlap is the generic picture of a coherent excitation passing a localized feature; the governing machinery differs. Paper B is nonlinear soliton scattering governed by the Gross-Pitaevskii (cubic NLS) equation with a high-velocity asymptotic split into reflected + transmitted solitons plus dispersion, whereas paper A is the linear classical dynamics of a winding string through a time-dependent orbifold (cosmological) singularity, demonstrating non-singular passage rather than any reflection/transmission decomposition, so no role-for-role mapping of the driving process holds.
